markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Link LoomBlock records to Project Knowledge entities, rich documents, assets, tags, and source spans. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LoomBlockKnowledgeBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LoomBlockKnowledgeBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: SQLite fully removed/unreachable from the Loom runtime path. (1) storage/mod.rs declares NO sqlite/locus_sqlite module (only postgres). (2) sqlx in Cargo.toml has NO 'sqlite' feature - a SqlitePool cannot be constructed in this crate. (3) The orphan storage/sqlite.rs (15x sqlx::sqlite/SqlitePool, would NOT compile) and storage/locus_sqlite.rs are physically removed via git rm. (4) StorageBackendKind enum has only Postgres; ControlPlaneStorageMode::resolve always returns PostgresPrimary and rejects non-postgres URLs. (5) AppState.storage is always Arc<dyn Database> backed by PostgresDatabase. NOTE: src/locus/sqlite_store.rs is misnamed legacy - it is a pure LocusOperation JSON parser with ZERO SQLite code (not a SQLite path); left untouched.

No parallel infrastructure. Bridge EXTENDS existing layers: knowledge_entities (entity_kind='loom_block' already a first-class kind, migration 0135) is the ProjectKnowledgeIndex authority; reuses KnowledgeStore::upsert_knowledge_entity (stable identity upsert), Database::append_kernel_event (EventLedger) with the pre-existing KnowledgeLoomBlockIndexed kernel event type; extends storage/loom.rs + api/loom.rs + the loom_blocks/loom_edges (migration 0013) store rather than forking it.

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 2ee98c28+58b773a5.

findings: ORPHAN-BLOCK (fixed): loom_blocks.block_id is a bare TEXT PK with no format CHECK and CreateLoomBlockRequest.block_id is caller-supplied. An empty/whitespace-surrounded id passes loom_blocks but FAILS the knowledge_entities.entity_key CHECK (btrim=value AND <>''), so the fail-closed bridge would refuse it AFTER the block row was inserted, leaving an orphan block outside authority.

NON-ATOMIC-BRIDGE (accepted, self-healing): bridge does 3 sequential awaits (entity upsert, event append, bridge upsert) not in one transaction. A crash between event-append and bridge-upsert leaves an orphan entity+event with no bridge row; the next bridge call is idempotent (re-upserts entity, appends a new receipt, upserts bridge) and self-heals. No data loss; no duplicate authority rows.
INJECTION (clear): all writes are sqlx-parameterized; entity_key/display_name are bound params under CHECK constraints. No string interpolation of user input into SQL.
DEDUP-IMPORT-PATH (clear): import dedup_hit returns BEFORE the bridge call and points at the existing (already-bridged) block, so a duplicate import does not create an un-bridged block.
DOS (low): list_loom_block_knowledge_bridges has no LIMIT (workspace-scoped admin/debug read). get/bridge-on-create are O(1). Acceptable for the surface.
fixes: create_loom_block now validates the resolved block_id (reject empty or surrounding-whitespace) BEFORE insert, so an un-bridgeable block can never be created -> closes ORPHAN-BLOCK at the source. Proven by create_rejects_unbridgeable_block_id (asserts 5 bad ids rejected + zero blocks in the All view).

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-177 requires binding every LoomBlock to the ProjectKnowledgeIndex (knowledge_entities, entity_kind=loom_block) plus an EventLedger receipt so Loom resolves to PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rather than a parallel/SQLite store. The implementation delivers this with a real FK-enforced bridge table (migration 0292), a real PostgresDatabase implementation that upserts the knowledge entity, appends a real KNOWLEDGE_LOOM_BLOCK_INDEXED EventLedger receipt, and upserts an idempotent bridge row, all proven by real Handshake-managed-PostgreSQL integration tests. No SQLite, mock, or deferred-live escape is reachable from the required path.
findings: migrations/0292_loom_block_knowledge_bridge.sql: real FK-enforced bridge table loom_block_knowledge_bridge(block_id PK -> loom_blocks ON DELETE CASCADE, entity_id -> knowledge_entities, index_event_id NOT NULL -> kernel_event_ledger ON DELETE RESTRICT, UNIQUE(entity_id)). EventLedger receipt is mandatory, matching the MT's PostgreSQL+EventLedger authority requirement.

postgres.rs:4825+ bridge_loom_block_to_knowledge is a real impl: (1) get_loom_block fail-closes on missing/foreign block, (2) upsert_knowledge_entity with stable identity (workspace, loom_block, block_id) reusing the existing KnowledgeStore, (3) appends a real KernelEventType::KnowledgeLoomBlockIndexed EventLedger event via append_kernel_event, (4) upserts the bridge row pointing at the stored event_id. No placeholder/stub on the live path.

Verified prerequisites pre-exist and are real: kernel/mod.rs:274 KnowledgeLoomBlockIndexed event type; storage/knowledge.rs:294/844 KnowledgeEntityKind::LoomBlock -> "loom_block". The bridge EXTENDS existing infrastructure, no parallel store.

sub_rule_1: storage/mod.rs trait defaults for bridge_loom_block_to_knowledge/get/list return StorageError::NotImplemented, but these are UNREACHABLE in production. grep confirms PostgresDatabase (postgres.rs:4333 impl super::Database) is the SOLE Database implementor and overrides all three; AppState.storage is always Arc<dyn Database> backed by PostgresDatabase. The NotImplemented defaults are not on any reachable required-behavior path. No deferred-live escape.

sub_rule_2: tests/loom_knowledge_bridge_tests.rs runs against REAL Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support (managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, port 5544, no Docker/no SQLite/no mock; skips loudly when PG binaries absent, panics on any other failure). Tests assert the knowledge entity exists with KEN- id + correct entity_key, the bridge row points at a real KNOWLEDGE_LOOM_BLOCK_INDEXED ledger event aggregated on the entity, idempotency (1 entity + 1 bridge row, fresh receipt on re-index), get/list read-back, and fail-closed on missing block. Real managed resource touched.

SQLite removal verified live: grep confirms storage/mod.rs declares no sqlite/locus_sqlite module; orphan files src/storage/sqlite.rs and locus_sqlite.rs are physically absent; source-level regression guard test (sqlite_is_unreachable_from_the_loom_runtime_path) asserts no module decl, no orphan files, sqlx lacks the sqlite feature, and no SqlitePool/storage::sqlite reference in Loom source. Matches packet forbidden-SQLite (DEC-008).

api/loom.rs: create_loom_block, import_loom_asset, and ensure_edge_target_exists (auto-created link/tag targets) all fail-closed bridge after creation, and GET /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:id/knowledge exposes the binding (404 loom_block_not_bridged when absent). Backend navigation API present for the authority binding.

Adversarial fix (58b773a5): create_loom_block now rejects empty/surrounding-whitespace block_id at the source (postgres.rs:3405+) because the id becomes the knowledge_entities entity_key (btrim CHECK), closing the orphan-block window; proven by create_rejects_unbridgeable_block_id (5 bad ids rejected, zero blocks in All view).

Spec alignment: MT cites Master Spec 10.12 #9.1.1 (WP-009 PostgreSQL+EventLedger authority supersession). Current v02.194 02-system-architecture.md lines 44/2006/2267 confirm the current authority direction is Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger 'from the start ... avoiding stale SQLite, Docker, or outside-app fallback paths'. Legacy base-research SQLite-as-index narrative (lines 336/1158-1283) is superseded by this direction; the MT does not contradict current spec.

Aside (non-blocking): self-review reviewer is KERNEL_BUILDER backend delegate (self-cert), and the NON-ATOMIC-BRIDGE note (3 sequential awaits, not one transaction) is accepted as self-healing/idempotent on the next bridge call with no duplicate authority rows or data loss; proof is real so self-cert is not disqualifying per governance wind-down.
